title: 中國富豪關閉旗下喺澳洲既醫院 涉8000床位 迫澳洲政府俾財政資助

replies.msg:
https://twitter.com/anderscorr/status/1244684461154975751?s=21

原新聞
間野係有財政壓力既
Chinese billionaire Liu Dian Bo bought Healthe Care for $900 million in 2015. Now, the company, which 9News understands was already facing financial pressure, is putting the heat on federal and state government for help.
"We have received no firm proposals," the company's letter to staff said.
"Regrettably, this has driven us to these immediate actions."
Other private hospitals are threatening to follow suit.
"We need it to be solved by the end of this weekend or hospitals will start to close," Australian Private Hospitals Association Michael Roff told 9News.
⋯⋯

Health Minister Greg Hunt told 9News sacking nurses during this crisis "is an astonishing breach of faith".
The national cabinet is expected to sign off on a deal regarding private hospital beds tomorrow.

https://www.9news.com.au/national/coronavirus-healthe-care-private-hospital-company-stands-down-800-staff/69040a83-b020-4b47-a543-24e2348788d4

quote.msg:
我睇 theguardian
佢係話Australia’s private hospitals face closure after coronavirus causes elective surgery ban
文中都有講話佢可以幫手處理武肺，但要政府比錢

link：https://www.theguardian.com/world/2020/mar/28/australias-private-hospitals-face-closure-after-coronavirus-causes-elective-surgery-ban

唔知係咪哩間

replies.msg:
應該係

The company’s chief executive, Steve Atkins, told Guardian Australia the prime minister, Scott Morrison, caught private hospitals by surprise when he announced on Wednesday that non-urgent elective surgery would be banned from midnight that night.

He said it would not be difficult for private providers to do a deal with public hospitals to support them during the crisis, but they needed urgent government help so they could stay open until they were required.

Atkins said Healthe Care, which owns 34 hospitals and employs 8,000 staff in eastern states, generated about 70% of its revenue from elective surgery. The other 30% had begun to decline as people stayed home and private hospitals responded to calls to save dwindling supplies of protective equipment for urgent cases.

應該係

The company’s chief executive, Steve Atkins, told Guardian Australia the prime minister, Scott Morrison, caught private hospitals by surprise when he announced on Wednesday that non-urgent elective surgery would be banned from midnight that night.

He said it would not be difficult for private providers to do a deal with public hospitals to support them during the crisis, but they needed urgent government help so they could stay open until they were required.

Atkins said Healthe Care, which owns 34 hospitals and employs 8,000 staff in eastern states, generated about 70% of its revenue from elective surgery. The other 30% had begun to decline as people stayed home and private hospitals responded to calls to save dwindling supplies of protective equipment for urgent cases.
